text.title: Figma Comment Triage Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Figma Comment Triage Protocol. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every open comment is classified into exactly one of FIX / DECIDE / ANSWER / IGNORE; multi-tag or 'other' classification is rejected.
rule.rationale: Open taxonomies erode discipline; closed enums make sweeps comparable across days and trainable for agents.

rule.statement: Each row carries a named owner (handle or role-with-rotation); 'team' / 'we' / empty are rejected.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership produces orphan rows; named owners get pinged when SLA elapses.

rule.statement: Each category has an SLA: FIX 48h, DECIDE 7d, ANSWER 24h, IGNORE same-day. Rows past SLA surface as a warning on next sweep.
rule.rationale: Without SLA the queue depth grows silently; SLA + warning create a feedback loop the designer can act on.

rule.statement: Every DECIDE row produces a row in the design-decision-log (or links to an existing log row); decisions must not live in comments long-term.
rule.rationale: Figma comments are ephemeral; the log is canonical. Without routing, decisions evaporate when the file is archived.

rule.statement: Every FIX row carries a link to the eng ticket or commit that addresses it; resolved-without-link is rejected.
rule.rationale: Resolved-without-link rows fail audit; the link makes the fix traceable to actual code.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-figma-comment-triage-protocol.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
